steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The function is $f(x)=3{,}600(0.28)^{x/12}$ where $x$ is months. Each time $x$ increases by 12, the exponent increases by 1, so the value is multiplied by 0.28 every year. The equipment keeps 28% of its value each year, meaning it loses $100\%-28\%=72\%$ per year. The question asks for $p$ where the equipment decreases by $p\%$ each year.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '2': The base is 0.28, not 0.02. ✗
  • Choice B — '10': No part of the function gives 10. ✗
  • Choice C — '28': 0.28 is the fraction kept each year, not the fraction lost. ✗
  • Choice D — '72': $100\%-28\%=72\%$ lost per year, so $p=72$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
